---
applyTo: "sdk/*/arm-*/**"
description: "Azure ARM/management SDK review rules covering TypeSpec generation, version consistency, and management-specific patterns."
---
# Management SDK Review ‚Äî ARM Package Patterns

**Scope:** Public API + tool validation for `@azure/arm-*` packages. Review `src/`, `review/*.api.md`, README, CHANGELOG, samples, `snippets.spec.ts`. Skip implementation internals, private methods. Only flag issues introduced by this PR.

ARM packages are auto-generated from TypeSpec or Swagger. Review focuses on public API surface and rule-based validations.

## Version Consistency (Tool Validation)
Package version must align across:
- `package.json` ‚Äî source of truth
- `src/api/*Context.ts` (e.g., `managedOpsContext.ts`) ‚Äî discover the appropriate `*Context.ts` file first
- `CHANGELOG.md` top heading

Cross-check code references in README, `snippets.spec.ts`, and public API. Inconsistent versions ‚Üí critical tool issue.

**Version rules:**
- First package version must be preview (regardless of API versions)
- Preview API versions can only ship in preview package versions
- No alpha versions anywhere (`CHANGELOG.md`, context files, `package.json`)
- First CHANGELOG entry is hard-coded template ‚Äî ignore content, only check version matches

## Breaking Changes
Breaking changes are acceptable in mgmt SDKs with proper CHANGELOG documentation.

**Root-cause analysis:** Trace breaking changes to their source:
- API version change in spec ‚Üí expected, document in changelog
- TypeSpec migration artifact ‚Üí may be approved pattern, check with architects
- Emitter/tooling bug ‚Üí report to autorest.typescript
- Do NOT flag cosmetic changes: `XxxOptionalParams‚ÜíXxxOptions`, response wrapper removal

**Changelog alignment:** Public API changes in `review/*.api.md` must be documented:
- New exports ‚Üí Features Added
- Removed/renamed exports ‚Üí Breaking Changes with migration guidance

| Case | Action |
|------|--------|
| Client name changed | ‚ùå Not allowed. Use `@clientName` decorator in TypeSpec spec repo |
| Stable versions removed from `KnownVersions` | ‚ùå Not allowed (preview versions may be removed) |
| `subscriptionId` or similar constructor params removed | ‚öÝÔ∏è Flag, discuss migration path on spec side |
| Last major release <6 months ago | ‚öÝÔ∏è Flag frequent breakage, discuss justification |
| Method parameters re-ordered | ‚ùå Restore original order. Use `@override` decorator in TypeSpec |

## Naming Validation (Spec Issues)
Flag patterns indicating spec problems:
- **`_N` suffixes** (e.g., `Resource_1`) ‚Üí duplicated models, use `@clientName`
- **`AutoGenerated` suffixes** ‚Üí duplicated Swagger models, merge in spec
- **`_` prefixes on enums** ‚Üí use `@clientName` for better name

## Type Safety
- Avoid `unknown` in return types
- Avoid `unknown` in public models (exception: `ErrorAdditionalInfo`)
- No `void` return for create/update/get/list operations (`void` acceptable for delete/upgrade/reset actions)
- `any` is acceptable in mgmt SDKs ‚Äî do NOT flag
- `undocumented` tags on public API are acceptable ‚Äî do NOT flag

## Exports
- New symbols in `src/index.ts` must appear in `review/{package-name}-node.api.md`
- `ae-forgotten-export` warnings indicate missing exports ‚Äî usually generation tool bug
- Avoid exports clashing with web/DOM types (`Request`, `Response`, `Event`)

## Samples & Tests
- Samples are auto-generated ‚Äî only comment on syntax issues found while checking `src/` references
- Do NOT comment on style, formatting, documentation, or whitespace
- Do NOT comment on implementation internals (private methods, internal helpers)

## Issue Types
**üî¥ Tool Issue:** Generation bug ‚Üí fix + report to [autorest.typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues)
**üî¥ Design Issue:** API problem ‚Üí `@clientName`/`@override` in spec repo
**üîµ Suggestion:** Optional improvement
